Grad-Shafranov Explorer

Explore an axisymmetric MHD equilibrium by changing pressure, toroidal-field source, and shaping coils. The browser solve is a reduced fixed-boundary model; the exported TOML runs through tokamaker-jax for reproducible Python/JAX calculations.

J x B = grad(p) -> Delta* psi

Inputs

fixed boundary

Residual
psi range
Magnetic axis
Plasma shaping

Flux surfaces

step 90

Equation

axisymmetric MHD
Delta* psi = -mu0 R^2 p'(psi) - F(psi) F'(psi)

The left side is the elliptic Grad-Shafranov operator. The right side contains pressure-gradient drive and toroidal-field/current drive. Coils enter through the boundary field or source response.

Pressure

Increasing -dp/dpsi strengthens the plasma pressure source and broadens the flux well.

FF prime

Increasing -F dF/dpsi changes the toroidal-field contribution and current profile.

What to try

classroom prompts
Raise pressureflux well deepens
Raise coilsaxis shifts and edge shape changes
Increase elongationsurfaces stretch vertically
Change triangularityoutboard side becomes D-shaped

Run in tokamaker-jax

reproducible case
